parsedSituation: A US passport holder plans to arrive in Thailand on March 15 for a 28-day stay under the visa exemption program, working remotely for a US employer. They plan one border run to Malaysia.

visaOptions.name: Visa Exemption (Tourist)
visaOptions.summary: Best fit for your 28-day stay. No application required — granted on arrival. Extendable once for 30 days at a local immigration office.
cons: Land border runs capped at 2 per calendar year

visaOptions.name: Tourist Visa (TR)
visaOptions.summary: Overkill for 28 days but useful if you plan multiple trips and want a visa in your passport for cleaner entry.
cons: Must apply at consulate before travel
No practical advantage over exemption for short stays

recommendedAction.action: Enter on Visa Exemption — no pre-application required.
recommendedAction.deadline: Book onward ticket before March 15 departure
recommendedAction.rationale: Your 28-day stay is well within the 60-day visa exemption allowance for US passport holders. One border run to Malaysia is within the 2-per-year land crossing limit. Carry proof of onward travel due to stricter checks at Suvarnabhumi since January 2025.

documents: Valid US passport (6+ months validity beyond intended stay)
Proof of sufficient funds (20,000 THB per person, ~$550 USD)
notes: No health insurance required as of 2025
Onward ticket enforcement inconsistent but increasing at budget airline counters

borderRunAnalysis.limitsPerYear: 2 land border crossings per calendar year on visa exemption
recommendedCrossings: Padang Besar (Hat Yai → Padang Besar, train available)
borderRunAnalysis.enforcementPosture: Moderate — officers have discretion. Repeat crossings within same year may trigger questioning.
warnings: Exceeding 2 land crossings per year may result in denial of entry
Always carry proof of funds and onward ticket for border runs

items: Thailand extended visa exemption from 30 to 60 days for US passport holders, effective November 11, 2024.
Stricter onward ticket checks at Suvarnabhumi reported since January 2025.
watchItems: Onward ticket enforcement at Suvarnabhumi increasing — book before flying

confirmed.description: 60 days for air entry confirmed by Thai MFA and immigration portal.
20,000 THB per person confirmed on official immigration portal.

contested.description: Officially required but inconsistently enforced. Community reports suggest increasing scrutiny at Suvarnabhumi since early 2025.
contested.resolution: Carry onward ticket to avoid secondary screening. Tier 1 official requirement stands.

sourceCitations.claim: 60-day visa exemption for US passport holders
Land border crossing limit: 2 per year

deniedEntrySteps: Remain calm and ask the officer for the specific reason in writing
Contact the US Embassy in Bangkok: +66-2-205-4000
Do not re-attempt entry at the same crossing on the same day
contingency.overstayScenario: Overstaying in Thailand incurs a 500 THB/day fine, capped at 20,000 THB. Overstay exceeding 90 days results in a 1-year ban; over 1 year, a 3-year ban. Turn yourself in at immigration before departure to minimize consequences.

disclaimer: This report aggregates publicly available information. Verify all visa requirements with official sources before travel. Not legal advice.
